front: Figure: renamed the Gold Coast after a medieval Sahel kingdom, rather than keep the colonial name, upon independence
work: General / Giveaway Identifiers

front: Figure: was known in Ghana by the honorific "Osagyefo," meaning "the Redeemer"
work: General / Giveaway Identifiers

front: Event: hosted the first meeting of the "World Without the Bomb" group
work: General / Giveaway Identifiers

front: Institution: founded when Kwame Nkrumah broke from J.B. Danquah's United Gold Coast Convention (UGCC), where Nkrumah had served as General Secretary
work: Founding the CPP & the Road to Independence

front: Institution: campaigned under the slogan "self-government now" and the "Positive Action" program of strikes, boycotts, and civil disobedience
work: Founding the CPP & the Road to Independence

front: Institution: united trade unions, women's groups, and cocoa farmers into this party
work: Founding the CPP & the Road to Independence

front: Figure: campaigned under the slogan "Seek ye first the political kingdom, and all else shall be added unto you" for his new political party
work: Founding the CPP & the Road to Independence

front: Figure: while imprisoned at Fort James, led the Convention People's Party to victory in the 1951 election, wearing a cap marked "PG" for "prison graduate"
work: Founding the CPP & the Road to Independence

front: Figure: was released from prison by Governor Charles Arden-Clarke and asked to form a government, moving from prisoner to Prime Minister within 48 hours
work: Founding the CPP & the Road to Independence

front: Figure: his official residence changed from Fort James to Fort Christiansborg upon becoming head of government
work: Founding the CPP & the Road to Independence

front: Institution: convened by Kwame Nkrumah in opposition to the British-appointed Coussey Committee, presenting plans for strikes and boycotts
work: Founding the CPP & the Road to Independence

front: Event: delivered shortly before declaring independence, declared that freedom "is not something that one people can bestow on another as a gift"
work: Founding the CPP & the Road to Independence

front: Figure: was brought back to the country by George Alfred Grant
work: Founding the CPP & the Road to Independence

front: Institution: founded by Kwame Nkrumah to orient college students to his ideology
work: Founding the CPP & the Road to Independence

front: Figure: defeated J.B. Danquah in both the 1953 parliamentary election and the 1960 presidential election
work: Founding the CPP & the Road to Independence

front: Figure: negotiated with Governor Charles Arden-Clarke to secure his country's independence in 1957
work: Founding the CPP & the Road to Independence

front: Event: created Lake Volta and provided electricity to nearby workers upon its construction
work: The Akosombo Dam & the Volta River Project

front: Event: was built with Soviet financial assistance, creating the world's largest artificial lake by surface area
work: The Akosombo Dam & the Volta River Project

front: Figure: nationalized cocoa exports to help fund the Akosombo Dam's construction
work: The Akosombo Dam & the Volta River Project

front: Event: VALCO and its parent company Kaiser sponsored the aluminum-smelting project tied to this dam
work: The Akosombo Dam & the Volta River Project

front: Event: attempts to control rapidly rising cocoa prices fueled unrest that led to the creation of an opposition movement against Nkrumah
work: The Akosombo Dam & the Volta River Project

front: Figure: his 1966 ouster followed the national cocoa board's refusal to raise cocoa prices after a crop failure
work: The Akosombo Dam & the Volta River Project

front: Event: this dam's 1960s completion has been tied to the later growth of Accra's Agbogbloshie e-waste dump
work: The Akosombo Dam & the Volta River Project

front: Event: Togolese President Sylvanus Olympio called Nkrumah a "black imperialist" over Togo's resulting dependency on this dam for electricity
work: The Akosombo Dam & the Volta River Project

front: Figure: broke off his alliance with Togolese President Sylvanus Olympio over the geographic split of Olympio's Ewe ethnic group
work: The Akosombo Dam & the Volta River Project

front: Figure: permitted Amilcar Cabral to set up training camps in Ghana to prepare for Guinea-Bissau's independence struggle
work: The Akosombo Dam & the Volta River Project

front: Work: applies set-theoretic terms to argue against imperialism and lays out a philosophy of decolonization
work: Philosophical Writings: Consciencism

front: Work: connects philosophical idealism with a tiered society and materialism with an egalitarian one
work: Philosophical Writings: Consciencism

front: Work: cites Anton Wilhelm Amo to discard Descartes's notion of parallelism, seeking to unite Western, Islamic, and African philosophical traditions
work: Philosophical Writings: Consciencism

front: Work: examines the ever-changing structure of Consolidated Gold Fields, one of Harry Frederick Oppenheimer's companies
back: Neo-Colonialism: The Last Stage of Imperialism (Kwame Nkrumah)
work: Philosophical Writings: Consciencism

front: Work: argues that the chieftaincy/title system was the element of traditional African society easiest to reconcile with egalitarian values
work: Philosophical Writings: Consciencism

front: Figure: was criticized by Paulin Hountondji for grounding his "Philosophical Consciencism" in Africans' environment and living conditions
work: Philosophical Writings: Consciencism

front: Institution: headed by Kwame Nkrumah, sought a closely-integrated socialist Pan-African federation in rivalry with the Monrovia Group
work: Pan-Africanism & International Diplomacy

front: Event: built by Nkrumah for a meeting of the Organization of African Unity, which he helped found
work: Pan-Africanism & International Diplomacy

front: Figure: was almost assassinated during a 1964 meeting with Chinese premier Zhou Enlai
work: Pan-Africanism & International Diplomacy

front: Institution: an attempted transnational Pan-African federation joining Nkrumah with Ahmed Sekou Toure and Modibo Keita
work: Pan-Africanism & International Diplomacy

front: Law: allowed arrest and jailing for up to five years without trial, driving the National Liberation Movement to form the opposition United Party
work: Preventive Detention, the Dawn Broadcast, and the 1966 Coup

front: Event: a radio address in which Nkrumah publicly criticized corruption within his own party
work: Preventive Detention, the Dawn Broadcast, and the 1966 Coup

front: Event: John Evans Atta Mills began a tradition in 2009 of reading this speech annually on Nkrumah's birthday
work: Preventive Detention, the Dawn Broadcast, and the 1966 Coup

front: Work: Nkrumah was the main inspiration for the protagonist of this Peter Abrahams novel, published a year before Nkrumah took power
work: Preventive Detention, the Dawn Broadcast, and the 1966 Coup

front: Event: after this failed assassination attempt against Nkrumah, he had several of his own ministers (including H.H. Cofie-Crabbe, Ako Adjei, and Tawia Adamafio) arrested and tried
work: Preventive Detention, the Dawn Broadcast, and the 1966 Coup

front: Institution: a seven-year plan organized by J.H. Mensah to lead the country into socialism
work: Preventive Detention, the Dawn Broadcast, and the 1966 Coup

front: Event: overthrew Kwame Nkrumah in a 1966 coup, widely believed to be CIA-backed, while he was on a state visit abroad
back: National Liberation Council (led by Joseph Ankrah)
work: Preventive Detention, the Dawn Broadcast, and the 1966 Coup

front: Figure: fled into exile in Guinea after his 1966 overthrow, where Sekou Toure made him honorary co-president
work: Preventive Detention, the Dawn Broadcast, and the 1966 Coup

front: Figure: met lifelong advisor George Padmore while a student at Lincoln University

front: Figure: invited W. E. B. Du Bois to work on an encyclopedia in Ghana; Du Bois died in the country

front: Figure: married Fathia Rizk, a Coptic Egyptian woman selected for him by Gamal Abdel Nasser

front: Figure: maintained a close platonic relationship with his secretary Erica Powell
